question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByPayerAccountIdAndValidStartNsOrderByConsensusTimestampAsc()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the query does not enforce a maximum result set size to perform a resource exhaustion attack by repeatedly querying (payerAccountId, validStartNs) pairs that return large lists, causing the JPA layer to load thousands of Transaction objects into memory and reorganizing service availability for legitimate history queries?
